name: Echo
what_it_does: High-performance Go web framework from LabStack. Provides a custom Context type with JSON binding, validation, and rendering helpers. Built on net/http with custom routing that's faster than Go's standard mux. Includes a rich middleware ecosystem (JWT, CORS, rate limiting, compression) and first-class OpenAPI support. Between Gin's popularity and Chi's minimalism.
use_cases: Build Go agent REST APIs with Echo's convenient JSON binding, validation, and response helpers
Implement agent authentication middleware using Echo's built-in JWT middleware package
Create CRUD agent services with Echo's data binding and validation integration
Build Go agent microservices with Echo's middleware ecosystem (CORS, logging, rate limiting, compression)
Integrate OpenAPI documentation for Go agent APIs using Echo's swagger middleware support
not_for: Teams wanting net/http standard middleware compatibility — Echo uses custom Context; net/http middleware requires adaptation
Projects requiring extreme performance — Fiber (fasthttp) is faster; Gin is comparable to Echo
Developers preferring minimal dependencies — Echo includes more built-in middleware than Chi
best_when: You want a well-rounded Go web framework with convenient JSON helpers, middleware, and validation between Gin's complexity and Chi's minimalism.
avoid_when: You need net/http middleware compatibility or maximum raw performance — Chi for compatibility, Fiber for performance.

auth.notes: Web framework — auth via Echo's JWT middleware or custom middleware.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits built-in (middleware available). Performance similar to Gin.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Echo's HTTPError type carries status code and message. Custom error handler configurable via e.HTTPErrorHandler. Binding errors return descriptive validation messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP framework — idempotency is application responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: Echo's c.Bind() reads the request body once — calling Bind() twice on the same context returns empty result; save bound data before additional processing
Echo's custom Context type is not directly compatible with net/http Handlers — wrapping net/http handlers requires echo.WrapHandler(); standard middleware requires adaptation
Echo v4 to v5 (in development) has API changes — avoid mixing v4 and v5 patterns; check which version documentation is for
Validator registration: Echo requires registering a custom validator with e.Validator — Echo doesn't validate by default; binding without registered validator silently skips validation
Group middleware applies to all routes in the group including those added after the middleware — add middleware before route definitions within a group
Error handler must be set before any route registration — e.HTTPErrorHandler set after routes are added may not apply to all routes in some configurations

security_score_components.security_notes: Go memory safety. TLS via standard net/http. Validate all user input via Echo's binding. Custom Context prevents direct net/http exploits but requires echo-specific security practices.
